_note: Offline FALLBACK protocol content for server/routes/advise.js (agent KB-MOCK). Served when Rares' knowledge-service is unreachable / RARES_KB_URL unset / upstream error / empty guidance (context/decisions.md D1). Keyed by our Incident.category vocabulary (CONTRACTS §2). Coverage mirrors Rares' four domains (structural_collapse/USAR, casualty_triage/START, water_sanitation/Sphere, shelter_disease/PAHO-WHO) so the advisory panel looks identical whether or not his box is up. Operational protocol for TRAINED RESPONDERS ONLY — never patient medical advice (PRD §9).
standard_caution: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.

steps: Enforce periodic silence on site (all machinery and voices stop) to listen for trapped survivors.
Size up structural stability before any entry — do not enter unstable structures; shore or crib voids before committing responders.
Mark searched areas with standard USAR/INSARAG markings to prevent duplicate searches across shifts.
Triage every extricated casualty with START; anticipate crush injuries after prolonged entrapment.
Log void spaces and voice-contact locations with time and exact position for the next shift.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.
Aftershocks: agree on an evacuation signal and safe assembly point before starting work.

steps: Heavy machinery operates under USAR direction only — never lift-and-pull on a structure that may hold live victims.
Enforce silence periods before machinery starts at any site with possible trapped survivors.
Confirm shoring and void assessment are complete before applying any load to the structure.
Stage fuel, spotters and operator rotation before committing equipment to a multi-hour extrication.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.
One trained banksman/spotter controls each machine near a live-victim void.

steps: Apply START triage: direct the walking wounded aside first, then assess respirations, perfusion and mental status to sort immediate / delayed / minor / expectant.
Prioritize casualties freed after prolonged entrapment — crush-syndrome risk rises sharply after release.
Route red (immediate) casualties to the nearest facility with real capacity, not simply the nearest facility.
Record the name/description and destination of every evacuated casualty for family reunification.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.
Sorting and prioritization only — treatment decisions belong to qualified clinicians on scene.

steps: Minimum survival allocation: target ~15 L of water per person per day (Sphere).
Chlorinate distributed water; target ~0.5 mg/L free residual chlorine at the point of delivery.
If people are drinking from rivers or unknown sources, distribute purification tablets with instructions immediately.
Separate defecation areas from water sources; site latrines at least 30 m from any water point and downhill of it.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.
Reports of untreated-water consumption → flag the health lead for a diarrhoeal-disease watch.

steps: Target a minimum of ~3.5 m² covered living space per person; avoid dense open-floor crowding that accelerates disease spread.
Register occupants on entry (headcount, unaccompanied minors, medical needs) for tracking and reunification.
In crowded shelters with low vaccination coverage, prioritize measles vaccination and isolate suspected measles/cholera cases (PAHO/WHO).
Scale water, latrines and handwashing points with occupancy — apply the WASH minimums as headcount grows.
shelter.source_label: Sphere shelter standards + PAHO/WHO shelter disease-control guidance (condensed)
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.
Set up a triage/isolation corner away from the main sleeping area before an outbreak, not after.

steps: Distribute through registered lists per shelter/zone to avoid duplicate and missed households.
Prioritize infants, pregnant women, the elderly and people with medical dietary needs.
Provide ready-to-eat rations where cooking water or fuel is not assured.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.

steps: Establish and mark a perimeter; assign one access-control point.
Report gas leaks, fire and unstable structures up the chain before committing responders inside the perimeter.
Isolate suspected gas or live electrical hazards before any powered tools are used.
Reassess the zone after every significant aftershock.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.

steps: Size up the scene and confirm it is safe before committing responders; establish a marked perimeter and one access point.
Identify the primary need (rescue, medical, water, shelter) and report it up the chain with location and headcount.
Apply the relevant humanitarian standard for the confirmed need; escalate to a specialist team where one exists.
Reassess after every significant aftershock and hand off with a written status for the next shift.
cautions: Operational protocol for trained responders — not medical advice or patient diagnosis.
